name: Asaptic Labs
description: Asaptic Labs is the umbrella research-and-operating brand for AI agents at three hard crossings: post-quantum security, hardware, and physical-world human care.

crossings.label: × post-quantum security
crossings.summary: The transition from classical to post-quantum cryptography is not a library swap. AI agents sign on behalf of principals, delegate authority in chains, and operate across payment rails that will outlive today's signature schemes. Asaptic Labs works on the cryptographic infrastructure agents need before Q-Day — focusing on how authority is delegated, verified, and revoked across a distributed agent stack.

crossings.label: × hardware
crossings.summary: Embodied agents cannot undo a physical action. There is no rollback in the physical world. This single constraint separates hardware-grounded AI from every other application domain. Asaptic Labs works on the accountability architecture for agents whose decisions leave physical traces: audit trails, override logs, constraint embeddings, and the governance layer that regulators and operators can actually inspect.

crossings.label: × physical-world human care
crossings.summary: Regulated care — eldercare, clinical support, rehabilitation — is the hardest crossing. Consent, dignity, and liability are not software abstractions here; they are enforceable obligations attached to real people in real rooms. Asaptic Labs works on the accountability and safety layer for agents that operate in care environments.

keyEssays.title: The override log as the real moat
Agent identity in the post-quantum transition
Why physical-world care is the hardest crossing

scope: AI agents at three crossings — post-quantum security · hardware · physical-world human care
